steps.title: Turn the condition into an intersection
steps.content:
The condition $f(a)=-9a$ means
$$\sqrt{6a+7}=-9a$$
So graph $y=\sqrt{6x+7}$ and $y=-9x$ in Desmos. The $x$-coordinate of their intersection is the value of $a$.

steps.title: Read and confirm the valid intersection
steps.content:
Desmos shows the intersection at $x=-\frac{7}{27}$. This also fits the sign rule: the square root is nonnegative, so $-9a$ must be nonnegative and $a\le0$. Check choice B:
$$\sqrt{6\left(-\frac{7}{27}\right)+7}=\sqrt{\frac{49}{9}}=\frac{7}{3}$$
and
$$-9\left(-\frac{7}{27}\right)=\frac{7}{3}$$
Both sides match, so B is correct.
